skill.name: specdd-cli
skill.description: Guides agents to use the specdd CLI for SpecDD project setup, framework updates, spec discovery, inspection, and linting.
skill.summary: Use the specdd CLI to find, inspect, and lint SpecDD project specifications before coding.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography patterns, but the reviewed file is Markdown documentation for CLI usage, not executable Ruby code. The command findings are mostly false positives from inline code and shell examples, while the real risk is intentional local command execution and filesystem interaction through the specdd CLI.

medium_findings.title: Intentional Local CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to check for and run the specdd command, including resolve, inspect, lint, init, and update workflows. This is legitimate for a CLI helper skill, but it can execute local binaries and should be used only in trusted project workspaces.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Markdown clearly directs agents to invoke specdd commands. The intent is legitimate and bounded, but local command execution is a real operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem-Modifying Setup And Update Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill documents specdd init and specdd update, which can create target directories or update framework bootstrap files. The instructions require explicit operator requests for these actions, reducing but not eliminating filesystem modification risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly states that init can create directories and update can change framework files. It also includes clear operator-consent boundaries.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The repeated Ruby backtick execution alerts come from Markdown inline code spans and fenced command examples. No executable Ruby source, dynamic command construction, or hidden script was found in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed artifact is SKILL.md, and the flagged tokens are Markdown code formatting. No evidence of Ruby execution exists in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text-Matching False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts appear to match ordinary SpecDD wording and Markdown content rather than cryptographic API usage. No hashing, encryption, or credential handling code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are documentation text about SpecDD behavior and formats. There is no cryptographic implementation or algorithm selection to assess.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Alert Is Documented Local Path Resolution
low_findings.description: The path traversal alert is caused by documentation that resolve follows explicit local paths beginning with relative or absolute path syntax. This describes project-local spec resolution, not code that accepts untrusted path input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line documents CLI behavior and has no executable path handling code. It remains relevant as filesystem scope guidance for users.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: No suspicious instructions to override system behavior, skip review, claim pre-approval, or alter the audit process were found in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the full Markdown file found normal CLI guidance and safety boundaries. No evidence found for prompt injection language.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Invocation Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill guides agents to run local specdd commands. This is expected for the skill, but marketplace users should understand it depends on a trusted installed CLI.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command guidance is explicit and repeated. The security impact depends on the local specdd binary and workspace trust.

dangerous_patterns.title: Project File Creation Or Update Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents commands that initialize or update SpecDD framework files. These actions are bounded to explicit operator requests, but they can change files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented behavior includes creating a target directory and changing bootstrap files. The file also states these commands should not be routine.

content.user_title: Explore SpecDD Projects with the CLI
content.value_statement: SpecDD projects can be hard to navigate when specs are distributed across directories. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use the specdd CLI for focused discovery, inspection, and linting.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether the specdd CLI is available before using it.
Guides resolve commands for target-specific SpecDD context discovery.
Guides inspect commands for mapping specs and selected sections.
Guides lint commands after creating or editing .sdd files.
Documents init and update workflows that require explicit operator requests.
Explains when CLI output is not enough for write authority.
limitations: Requires an existing specdd CLI installation and does not install it automatically.
Applies only to projects with a .specdd/bootstrap.md file unless adoption is requested.
Does not replace direct reading of governing specs for exact contract text.
Can produce broad output when depth or section filters are too permissive.

use_cases.title: Find Relevant Specs Before Coding
use_cases.description: Use resolve on a concrete file or directory to identify the SpecDD context that should guide an implementation change.

use_cases.title: Review Spec Coverage In A Subtree
use_cases.description: Use inspect with selected sections to understand which .sdd files govern a feature area before planning work.

use_cases.title: Validate Edited Specification Files
use_cases.description: Use lint on edited .sdd files and nearby directories to catch syntax and parser diagnostics before reporting completion.

prompt_templates.title: Find Context For A File
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the SpecDD CLI to find the specs relevant to this file: [path]. Summarize the governing context before editing.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect A Feature Area
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the SpecDD specs under [directory] and show the Purpose, Structure, Must, and Tasks sections that matter for my change.

prompt_templates.title: Lint Recent Spec Edits
prompt_templates.prompt: I edited [spec path]. Use specdd lint on the narrowest path first, then lint the relevant directory if needed.

prompt_templates.title: Plan With Filtered Spec Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Resolve SpecDD context for [target] with focused sections and limited depth. Read the governing specs needed for write authority, then propose an implementation plan.

output_examples.output: A concise summary of the relevant project, directory, and same-basename specs, followed by the specs that still need direct reading.
A tree-shaped overview of discovered .sdd files with the requested sections summarized for planning.
A validation report that states whether lint passed, lists visible diagnostics if present, and names any remaining verification gap.

best_practices: Run resolve or inspect from the project root when possible.
Use section filters and limited depth to keep context focused.
Reread governing specs directly before making changes that need write authority.
anti_patterns: Do not use init or update unless the operator explicitly requests setup or framework updates.
Do not treat resolve output alone as permission to modify files.
Do not default to broad recursive file reads when the CLI can answer the context question.

faq.question: Does this skill install the specdd CLI?
faq.answer: No. It checks for the command and reports when CLI exploration is unavailable.

faq.question: Can it be used in any repository?
faq.answer: It is intended for repositories that already contain .specdd/bootstrap.md.

faq.question: When should resolve be used?
faq.answer: Use resolve when there is a concrete file, directory, or .sdd target that needs relevant SpecDD context.

faq.question: When should inspect be used?
faq.answer: Use inspect to map specs in a subtree or review selected sections before authoring or planning.

faq.question: Does CLI output grant write authority?
faq.answer: No. The skill says to read governing specs directly when exact contract text is needed.

faq.question: What security warning applies?
faq.answer: The skill intentionally runs local CLI commands and can guide file-changing setup commands when explicitly requested.
